citation: Gat et al., Discrete Flow Matching, 2024
use: DFM permits arbitrary couplings and learned endpoint posteriors. A7 keeps the absorbing DFM path but models a graph-level count latent before constrained token transitions.

citation: Miller et al., FlowMM: Generating Materials with Riemannian Flow Matching, 2024
use: Crystal generation must respect permutation symmetry; A7 treats composition as a permutation-invariant graph quantity and only then assigns types to sites.
